summary: rankaj bundled a JavaScript payload that reads trusted AI service configuration and exfiltrates stolen data to an attacker-controlled webhook.
details: Antiy CERT sample analysis (classified Trojan/OpenClaw.PolySkill): the malicious code resides in a JavaScript file (index.js, MD5 2444b3ab5de42fcca22e6025cf018e3b) inside the skill archive and exfiltrates data, including trusted configuration sources for paid AI services (such as Claude and OpenAI), to a webhook. This 'environmental parasitism' technique exploits the agent's elevated privileges (reading .env files) to send credentials directly to webhooks without complex C2 communication.
